# Practical 4  Encoding, Dummification, Scaling

# Load dataset
df <- read.csv("F:/External Practical Exam 2026 (March)/1. Data Science/Pract 4 Encoding, Dummification, Scaling/Advancements & Research Survey.csv")

# Encoding

df_encoded <- df

df_encoded[] <- lapply(df_encoded, function(col){
  if(is.character(col) || is.factor(col)){
    as.numeric(as.factor(col))
  }else{
    col
  }
})

head(df_encoded)


# Dummification

install.packages("fastDummies")
library(fastDummies)

df_dummy <- dummy_cols(df,
                       remove_first_dummy=TRUE,
                       remove_selected_columns=TRUE)

head(df_dummy)


# Scaling

num_cols <- sapply(df_dummy,is.numeric)

df_scaled <- df_dummy

df_scaled[num_cols] <- scale(df_dummy[num_cols])

head(df_scaled)
column3:
# Practical 5  Hypothesis Testing (T-test and P-value), ANOVA




# Hypothesis Testing (T-test and P-value)

# Load CSV
df <- read.csv("F:/External Practical Exam 2026 (March)/1. Data Science/Pract 5 Hypothesis, ANOVA/New Tanmay.csv")

print("Dataset Loaded:")
print(head(df))

# Select binary columns
before <- df[,1]
after <- df[,2]

# Binary encoding meaning
cat("Binary Encoding Used:\n")
cat("1 = Yes\n")
cat("0 = No\n\n")

print("Null Hypothesis (H0): There is NO difference between Before and After.")
print("Alternative Hypothesis (H1): There IS a difference between Before and After.")

# Paired T-test
test_result <- t.test(before, after, paired=TRUE)

print(test_result)

# Decision based on P-value
cat("\nDecision:\n")

if(test_result$p.value <= 0.05){
cat("Reject H0 -> Significant difference found.\n")
cat("Interpretation: Treatment is effective.\n")
}else{
cat("Fail to Reject H0 -> No significant difference found.\n")
cat("Interpretation: Treatment has no measurable effect.\n")
}




# ANOVA

# Load CSV
data <- read.csv("F:/External Practical Exam 2026 (March)/1. Data Science/Pract 5 Hypothesis, ANOVA/New Tanmay.csv")

print("Dataset Loaded:")
print(head(data))

# Rename columns
colnames(data) <- c("ScienceTech","ResearchProjects")

# Convert to ANOVA format
values <- c(data$ScienceTech, data$ResearchProjects)

group <- c(rep("ScienceTech", length(data$ScienceTech)),
           rep("ResearchProjects", length(data$ResearchProjects)))

anova_data <- data.frame(values, group)

# Perform ANOVA
anova_result <- aov(values ~ group, data=anova_data)

summary(anova_result)

# Install and load ggplot
install.packages("ggplot2")
library(ggplot2)

# ggplot Boxplot
ggplot(anova_data, aes(x=group, y=values, fill=group)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title="ANOVA Comparison",
       x="Group",
       y="Values")
column4:
#Practical 6: Regression



# 1. Simple linear regression
install.packages("ggplot2")

library(ggplot2) 

data <- mtcars 
model <- lm(mpg ~ hp, data = data) 
summary(model) 
ggplot(data, aes(x = hp, y = mpg)) + 
  geom_point() + 
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", col = "blue") + 
  theme_minimal() 



# 2. Multiple linear regression
model_multi <- lm(mpg ~ hp + wt + cyl, data = data) 
summary(model_multi) 



# 3. Logistic regression
data <- iris
data$Species <- ifelse(data$Species == "setosa", 1, 0) 
model_logistic <- glm(Species ~ Sepal.Length + Sepal.Width, data = data, family = binomial) 
summary(model_logistic) 



# 4. Bayesian estimation 
install.packages("bayesreg") 
library(bayesreg) 
bayes_model <- bayesreg(mpg ~ hp + wt, data = mtcars, prior = "ridge") 
summary(bayes_model) 



# 5. Ridge regression 
install.packages("glmnet")

library(glmnet) 
x <- model.matrix(mpg ~ hp + wt, data = mtcars)[, -1] # Remove intercept 
y <- mtcars$mpg 
ridge_model <- glmnet(x, y, alpha = 0) 
lasso_model <- glmnet(x, y, alpha = 1) 
print(ridge_model) 
print(lasso_model) 



# 6. Model selection
install.packages("caret")
library(caret) 
train_control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5) 
cv_model <- train(mpg ~ hp + wt, data = mtcars, method = "lm", trControl = train_control) 
print(cv_model)
column5:
#Practical 7: Decision Tree and Random Forest




#Decision Tree

install.packages("rpart")
install.packages("rpart.plot")
install.packages("caret")

library(rpart)
library(rpart.plot)
library(caret)

data(iris)
set.seed(123)
train_index <- createDataPartition(iris$Species, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data <- iris[train_index, ]
test_data <- iris[-train_index, ]

tree_model <- rpart(Species ~ ., data = train_data, method = "class", 
                    control = rpart.control(minsplit = 10, cp = 0.01))

rpart.plot(tree_model, box.palette = "auto", nn = TRUE)#first diagram

predictions <- predict(tree_model, test_data, type = "class")
confusionMatrix(predictions, test_data$Species)

printcp(tree_model)
optimal_cp <-
  tree_model$cptable[which.min(tree_model$cptable[, "xerror"]), "CP"]
pruned_tree <- prune(tree_model, cp = optimal_cp)
rpart.plot(pruned_tree)#2 diagram

control <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10)
tuned_tree <- train(Species ~., 
                    data = iris,
                    method = "rpart",
                    trControl = control,
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(cp = seq(0.01, 0.1)))
print(tuned_tree)




#Random Forest

install.packages("randomForest")

library(randomForest)
data(iris)
head(iris)
set.seed(42)
trainIndex <- sample(1:nrow(iris), 0.8 * nrow(iris))
trainData <- iris[trainIndex, ]
testData <- iris[-trainIndex, ]
rf_model <- randomForest(Species ~ ., data = trainData)
print(rf_model)

library(caret)

predictions <- predict(rf_model, testData)
confusionMatrix(predictions, testData$Species)

rf_tuned <- randomForest(Species ~ ., data = trainData, ntree = 500, mtry = 2)
print(rf_tuned)


importance(rf_model)
varImpPlot(rf_model)

data(iris)
head(iris)

kmeans_re

cm

# Practical 9  Principal Component Analysis (PCA)


# Install and load required packages
install.packages("corrplot")
library(corrplot)

install.packages("ggcorrplot")
library(ggcorrplot)

install.packages("FactoMineR")
library(FactoMineR)

install.packages("factoextra")
library(factoextra)

# Load dataset
data <- read.csv("F:/External Practical Exam 2026 (March)/1. Data Science/Pract 9 Principal Component Analysis (PCA)/protein.csv")

# View structure
str(data)

# Check missing values
colSums(is.na(data))

# Select numerical columns (2 to 10)
numerical_data <- data[, 2:10]

# View first rows
head(numerical_data)

# Normalize data
data_normalized <- scale(numerical_data)

# View normalized data
head(data_normalized)

# Perform PCA
data_pca <- princomp(data_normalized)

# PCA summary
summary(data_pca)

# PCA loadings
data_pca$loadings[,1:2]

# Eigenvalues plot
fviz_eig(data_pca, addlabels = TRUE)

# PCA variable plot
fviz_pca_var(data_pca, col.var="black")

# Cos2 plot
fviz_cos2(data_pca, choice="var", axes=c(1,2))

# PCA variable plot with cos2 colors
fviz_pca_var(
  data_pca,
  col.var="cos2",
  gradient.cols=c("black","orange","green"),
  repel=TRUE
)
